title: STOP Wasting Credits & Master Lovable AI! (Vibe Coding Tutorial)

segments.text: I built this entire app with interface,
user authentication, payments, database,
and everything that you just saw with
just one prompt. And this isn't the only
thing that I've built. In the last
couple of months, guys, I've created
fully functional apps, AI SaaS
platforms, and websites, all just by
prompting. And it absolutely blew my
mind because with Lovable, you can build
literally anything that you want. You
just describe what you want, and Lovable
builds it for you. That means that you
you don't need to learn how to code, and
you can actually focus on more enjoyable
things. So, kind of like creating the
interface or thinking about how to
market your product. But the thing is,
with most people, they burn through
about 50 plus, sometimes even 100
different prompts, and they waste
hundreds of credits trying to figure all
of this out. They start promoting
without a plan, and then what happens
most of the time? They find themselves
getting frustrated, and then they give
up before they see what is actually
possible. And that is exactly what I am
going to fix for you guys in the next 10
minutes. So, what I'm asking is for you
guys just to stick around till the end
because I can guarantee you you are
going to love this. I'm going to show
you how to master Lovable, save your
credits, and build anything that
actually works. And instead of burning
through all of your credits, you By the
end of this video, guys, you will know
my exact strategy. And if you want all
of these prompts that we are going to be
using in today's video and more, then
all of those are in our free school
community. The link is in the
description below, guys. If you go
there, just click on it, and I can
guarantee you guys you are going to have
so much fun. There is also a link if you
want to get started with Lovable down
there. So, as you can see, there's a lot
of valuable things in the description
below. But right now, guys, let's not
waste any time. Let me show you how all
of this works. Okay, guys, so now it's
time to go over to the PC, okay? So, I'm
going to be building three different
things. So, a multi-page website, a
simple app, and then a full-blown SaaS
platform, okay? And you guys are going
to be learning how to do that in the
next 10 minutes, okay? So, I'm going to
walk you through exactly what I'm doing
and why so that you guys completely
understand everything, and by the end of
it, it's going to all seem so simple to
you, okay? So now, guys, let's start off
with the website because honestly, this
is where most people always mess up,
okay? So, I'm building a portfolio site
for creative agency. So now, here is
what 99% of people do, okay? They open
up Lovable, and they like, "Okay, hey,
build me a portfolio website." And then,
what usually happens after that? They
spend the next hour and 30 different
prompts to 50 different prompts just
trying to fix the color, the layout, and
also the navigation, and all of that
different stuff, okay? So, yeah, that is
credits that you are actually spending,
and I mean, that is something that
people don't want to hear. Then that
you're just wasting money, okay? So,
here's the smarter way that you can do
this, and I'm going to be doing it,
okay? So, I'm going to head over to
Godly real quick, okay? This is a site
where you can find really clean design
inspirations, okay? So, I'm scrolling
through here, and I'm looking for a
portfolio that has the exact vibe that
we want, okay? So, what about this one,

perfect. So, I'm going to screenshot
this real quick, and now watch what I'm
going to do. So, I'm pasting the

just saying, "Make it look like this."
I'm actually giving it everything that

it, "Build me a portfolio site for a
creative agency. I need a homepage with
a project showcase grid, an about
homepage, and also a contact form, which
is super important." And then, I'm going
to make it match this design style. So,
use this navigation layout, and then
keep the mobile responsive, and add a
project filtering, okay? So now, once
that's done, look at this. It's already
busy building everything out. The
navigation is clean, the project grid
matches what I showed it, and honestly,
we are like 80% done with the just one
prompt. Now, I'm sure you guys can
appreciate the efficiency there. So,
that's the difference between knowing
how to prompt and just winging it. Most
people always make mistakes when it just
comes to winging it. So now, here's a
cool thing that most people don't know
about, okay? So, see this visual edit
button down here in the bottom left? If
you want to change some text or tweak a
color, then I don't need to waste a
prompt on that. And that is just wasting
more money. So, I just click on this. I
click whatever element I want to change,
and then boom, it's basically done. I
can edit it right here, and I can change
the heading, I can adjust the button
color, and zero credits are used, so it
means I am saving money. And then, save
your prompts for all of the real stuff
because I mean, you are going to need
later on. So, you want to make sure that
you are spending it all wisely, okay?
And as you can see, guys, it is pretty
straightforward, okay? So now, guys,
let's build something that's a little
bit more complex. Let's build an app,
okay? So now, I'm going to build a food
ordering app. Nothing crazy, just
something that we could all basically
use. But the thing is, it needs to
actually work. Now, this is where people
really start wasting credits because
they try to build everything at once,
and that is always the issue. They
usually go at it like, "Build me a food
ordering app with user login and
restaurant listings and menus and a
shopping cart and checkout and payment
processing and order taking and an admin
dashboard and everything that you can
basically think of. They just throw it
all into the prompt. And then, Lovable
usually spits out something that is half
broken, and then you are spending the
next 50 prompts just trying to fix what
you initially started. Guys, please do
not do that, okay? Build everything
piece by piece, and then test it as you
go. You are kind of putting all of the
elements together so that you can create
this perfect picture. But you're not
going at it by just throwing the paint
on the canvas. You are going at it small
section by small section. Then, at the
end of it, your picture is going to look
even better, okay? So, for the first
prompt, "Build me a food ordering app,
okay? Start with the basic structure.
So, I need a homepage that shows the
restaurant listings, a restaurant detail
page, and a simple menu display. Use
Lovable Cloud for the backend, okay?"
And it's creating the homepage. The
restaurant cards are now showing up, and
I can click into a restaurant, and I can
see their menu. Now, for the second
prompt, guys, "Add a shopping cart
feature, So, users need to be able to
add menu items to their cart, and they
need to see what's in their cart, and
they need to adjust all of the
quantities." And as you can see, guys,
it is now adding the cart functionality.
And see, because we built the foundation
first, and then we made sure that it was
solid, this new feature just plugs right
in. There's no conflicts whatsoever,
there's no debugging on our side,
there's nothing that is breaking during
the process. So, let me test it. You
I'll add an item, I'll check the cart,
I'll adjust the quantity, and there we
go, guys. Perfect. So now, let's get
over to the third prompt, okay? So now,
we are going to integrate Stripe for
payments, which is the basic way to go,
and it's also the safest option, okay?
So, you are going to add a checkout page
where users can enter their payment
details and also complete the order. And
there we go, guys, just like that, we
can now accept payments on our platform.
And guys, you can see how much cleaner
that was overall. Why? Because we built
everything step by step. We tested each
piece, and now we have a working app
without spending hundreds of credits
just fixing the bugs that we created.
So, this is what I mean by building
everything piece by piece. You build
something, you test it, you then make
sure that it works, and then you add to
the next thing. Because when you try and
build everything in the beginning within
the space of just like 5 minutes, what's
going to end up happening is you're
going to have to spend the next hour
upon hour just trying to fix it. Whereas
this way, you are spending your time
wisely, you're also saving money, and
you are getting the exact result that
you want, okay? So now that you guys
have that, now here's where everything
gets really interesting, okay? We are
going to build a complex SaaS app, okay?
So, I'm talking about a complete project
management tool with dashboards, team
collaborations, task management, and
basically the whole nine yards, okay? So
now, this is where most people
completely lose it with the credits.
This is where all of the wasting really
happens. They think that they need 50
different prompts to build something
this complex. But here's the secret. You
start with one really solid prompt that
sets up the entire structure, and then
you add features one at a time. So,
here's my prompt, okay? I'm going to
say, "Build me a project management SaaS
platform. Core functionality includes
the task management, team collaboration,
and dashboard. I need these pages:
dashboard, project, task, team, and
settings. For the database structure, I
need tables for users, project tasks,
and team members. Use Lovable Cloud, and
then make sure that it's fully
responsive." And this is now us building
our foundation. So, you see this arrow
in the bottom left? I'm going to click
on integrations, and then we are going
to enable Supabase.
And this is basically going to handle
our entire database for us, okay? So,
I'm connecting our Supabase account
right now, and there we go. Our database
tables are being created automatically.
Users, projects, tasks, team members,
all of it is going to be set up. So now,
I can start adding all of the features
one by one, okay? So, the next prompt,
"Add the ability to create a new project
and assign them to team members." So,
you see the difference here? We didn't
try to build everything at once like we
said before. We instead focused on
building a strong foundation with one
good prompt at a time. We connected our
database properly, and now we're adding
features step by step, and each one
builds on top of the last one without
breaking anything in the chain, and
that's making our entire platform even
stronger, okay? So now, let me show you
something else that's going to save you
a ton of credits. So, every 10 or 15
prompts, you need to clean up your code,
and I am so serious about this. This is
something that really is going to change
it for you. So, your code gets messy as
you keep adding on stuff. And messy code
means more bugs down the line, which
means more credits that you are wasting
time to fix all of these things. So,
here's what you have to do. I have this
prompt saved in the text expander. So,
it says, "Review my codebase and tell me
what needs refactoring. Which files are
too big? Which components should be
split up? What order should I refactor
things in?" And I run this every 10 to
15 changes and Lovable tells me exactly
what gets messy and how to clean it all
up, okay? So, now we are on to the last
thing, guys, and that is going to save
you a ton of different credits, and it's
called the knowledge section, okay? And
most people skip over this, but I really
don't want you guys to do that. So,
here's what Lovable does, okay? You set
your project standards once, and then
Lovable automatically applies them to
everything that it builds. So, that
means no more repeating yourself in
every single prompt. So, you're going to
go into the bottom left add button.
You're going to click on knowledge, and
here's what you need to put in there,
okay? You're going to start with your
coding standards. You're going to tell
it to keep components modular, and
you're going to use clear naming
conventions, and you are going to
comment complex logic, okay? So, guys,
whatever structure you want, you want to
basically define it the right here,
okay? And then you are going to add your
exact color palette with hex codes, and
primary color hashtag 3 B82F6,
okay? Super important, let me just
repeat that, okay, guys? 3 B82F6. And
then secondary color hashtag is going to
be 8B5CF6.
So, the aim is, guys, you want to be
specific, okay? Otherwise, you'll end up
with 10 different shades of blue and
maybe even red, and then you're just
going to waste all of these prompts just
trying to fix something that you just
created by accident, okay? And include
your design systems, too. So, the
typography, the spacing, the border
radius, and also the button styles, all
of the design decisions that should stay
consistent. And if you have brand
guidelines, put those in here as well,
okay? So, that is the logo usage, the
tone, and any specific constraints that
you may have. And then, now, guys, let
me show you why all of this matters,
okay? So, I'm setting this up right now
for our project management app. I'm
defining the colors, defining the fonts,
the spacing, all of it, okay? And you
can immediately see that it's going to
make a big difference. So, now when I
add a new feature like a notification
page, watch what happens, okay? The
colors match everything perfectly, the
space is consistent, and it takes 5
minutes to do this before you start
building. So, it will save you dozens of
prompts down the line, and I can
guarantee you guys it's the best way to
go for this. So, now you don't need to
waste credits on Lovable. You can just
build anything from a simple website to
a complete data integrated software. And
guys, if you followed us all the way
until the end, I guarantee you that this
is the route that you're going to want
to go down, and you are going to create
some insane things. By the way, if you
have any ideas of things that you want

comments. And there we have it, guys.
You have the framework, but if you want
